description: Necessary for completeness; being an essential part of a whole. 'Monae' names what cannot be removed without breaking wholeness—the piece that completes.
sound_symbolism: The word connects from within. 'Mo' begins with nasal wholeness, the inner sense that a part belongs to something larger. 'Nae' grounds with necessary presence. Integral as felt completeness, the mindful recognition that this piece matters.
grammatical_notes: Functions as adjective: 'monae miona' (integral person). Related to 'noetha' (essential) but names a part's necessity to a whole; compare 'whoa' (whole).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Integral systems—understanding how parts connect to create functioning wholes.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Integral being—recognizing oneself as essential part of larger wholes.
